81→/**\n    82→ * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n    83→ * Trigger}.\n    84→ *\n    85→ * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n    86→ * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n    87→ * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n    88→ * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n    89→ * WindowAssigner}.\n    90→ *\n    91→ * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n    92→ * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n    93→ * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n    94→ * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n    95→ *\n    96→ * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n    97→ * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n    98→ * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n    99→ * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n   100→ */\n   101→@Internal\n   102→public class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n   103→        extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n   104→        implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n   105→\n   106→    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n   107→\n   108→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   109→    // Configuration values and user functions\n   110→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   111→\n   112→    protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n   113→\n   114→    private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n   115→\n   116→    private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n   117→\n   118→    private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n   119→\n   120→    /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n   121→    protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n   122→\n   123→    /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n   124→    protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n   125→\n   126→    /**\n   127→     * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n   128→     *\n   129→     * <ul>\n   130→     *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n   131→     *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n   132→     *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n   133→     * </ul>\n   134→     */\n   135→    protected final long allowedLateness;\n   136→\n   137→    /**\n   138→     * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n   139→     * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n   140→     * to this.\n   141→     */\n   142→    protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n   143→\n   144→    private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n   145→\n   146→    protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n   147→\n   148→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   149→    // State that is not checkpointed\n   150→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   151→\n   152→    /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n   153→    private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n   154→\n   155→    /**\n   156→     * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n   157→     * state is not mergeable.\n   158→     */\n   159→    private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n   160→\n   161→    /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n   162→    private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n   163→\n   164→    /**\n   165→     * This is given to the {@code InternalWindowFunction} for emitting elements with a given\n   166→     * timestamp.\n   167→     */\n   168→    protected transient TimestampedCollector<OUT> timestampedCollector;\n   169→\n   170→    protected transient Context triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n   171→\n   172→    protected transient WindowContext processContext;\n   173→\n   174→    protected transient WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext windowAssignerContext;\n   175→\n   176→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   177→    // State that needs to be checkpointed\n   178→    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n   179→\n   180→    protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n   181→\n   182→    /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n   183→    public WindowOperator(\n   184→            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n   185→            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n   186→            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n   187→            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n   188→            StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n   189→            InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n   190→            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n   191→            long allowedLateness,\n   192→            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n   193→\n   194→        super(windowFunction);\n   195→\n   196→        checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n   197→\n   198→        checkArgument(\n   199→                windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n   200→                \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n   201→\n   202→        this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n   203→        this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n   204→        this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\n   205→        this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\n   206→        this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\n   207→        this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\n   208→        this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\n   209→        this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\n   210→    }\n   211→\n   212→    @Override\n   213→    public void setup(\n   214→            StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\n   215→            StreamConfig config,\n   216→            Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\n   217→        super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\n   218→    }\n   219→\n   220→    @Override\n   221→    public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\n   222→        super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\n   223→    }\n   224→\n   225→    @Override\n   226→    public void open() throws Exception {\n   227→        super.open();\n   228→\n   229→        this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n   230→        timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n   231→\n   232→        internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\"window-timers\", windowSerializer, this);\n   233→\n   234→        triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n   235→        processContext = new WindowContext(null);\n   236→\n   237→        windowAssignerContext =\n   238→                new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n   239→                    @Override\n   240→                    public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n   241→                        return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n   242→                    }\n   243→                };\n   244→\n   245→        // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\n   246→        // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\n   247→        if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\n   248→            windowState =\n   249→                    (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\n   250→                            getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\n   251→        }\n   252→\n   253→        // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\n   254→        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n   255→\n   256→            // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\n   257→            if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n   258→                windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>) windowState;\n   259→            } else if (windowState != null) {\n   260→ @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   265→            final Class<Tuple2<W, W>> typedTuple = (Class<Tuple2<W, W>>) (Class<?>) Tuple2.class;\n   266→\n   267→            final TupleSerializer<Tuple2<W, W>> tupleSerializer =\n   268→                    new TupleSerializer<>(\n   269→                            typedTuple, new TypeSerializer[] {windowSerializer, windowSerializer});\n   270→\n   271→            final ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsStateDescriptor =\n   272→                    new ListStateDescriptor<>(\"merging-window-set\", tupleSerializer);\n   273→\n   274→            // get the state that stores the merging sets\n   275→            mergingSetsState =\n   276→                    (InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>>)\n   277→                            getOrCreateKeyedState(\n   278→                                    VoidNamespaceSerializer.INSTANCE, mergingSetsStateDescriptor);\n   279→            mergingSetsState.setCurrentNamespace(VoidNamespace.INSTANCE);\n   280→        }\n   281→    }\n   282→\n   283→    @Override\n   284→    public void close() throws Exception {\n   285→        super.close();\n   286→        timestampedCollector = null;\n   287→        triggerContext = null;\n   288→        processContext = null;\n   289→        windowAssignerContext = null;\n   290→    }\n   291→\n   292→    @Override\n   293→    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n   294→        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n   295→                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n   296→                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n   297→\n   298→        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n   299→        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n   300→\n   301→        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n   302→\n   303→        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n   304→            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n   305→\n   306→            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n   307→\n   308→                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n   309→                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n   310→                // actualWindow == window\n   311→                W actualWindow =\n   312→                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n   313→                                window,\n   314→                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n   315→                                    @Override\n   316→                                    public void merge(\n   317→                                            W mergeResult,\n   318→                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n   319→                                            W stateWindowResult,\n   320→                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n   321→                                            throws Exception {\n   322→\n   323→                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n   324→                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n   325→                                                        <= internalTimerService\n   326→                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n   327→                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n   328→                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n   329→                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n   330→                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n   331→                                                            + internalTimerService\n   332→                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n   333→                                                            + \" window: \"\n   334→                                                            + mergeResult);\n   335→                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n   336→                                            long currentProcessingTime =\n   337→                                                    internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n   338→                                            if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n   339→                                                    <= currentProcessingTime) {\n   340→                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n   341→                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"\n   342→                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n   343→                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n   344→                                                                + currentProcessingTime\n   345→                                                                + \" window: \"\n   346→                                                                + mergeResult);\n   347→                                            }\n   348→                                        }\n   349→\n   350→                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n   351→                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n   352→\n   353→                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n   354→\n   355→                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n   356→                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n   357→                                            triggerContext.clear();\n   358→                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n   359→                                        }\n   360→\n   361→                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n   362→                                        // state window\n   363→                                        windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n   364→                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n   365→                                    }\n   366→                                });\n   367→\n   368→                // drop if the window is already late\n   369→                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n   370→                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n   371→                    continue;\n   372→                }\n   373→                isSkippedElement = false;\n   374→\n   375→                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n   376→                if (stateWindow == null) {\n   377→                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n   378→                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n   379→                }\n   380→\n   381→                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n   382→                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n   383→\n   384→                triggerContext.key = key;\n   385→                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n   386→\n   387→                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n   388→\n   389→                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n   390→                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n   391→                    if (contents != null) {\n   392→                        emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n   393→                    }\n   394→                }\n   395→\n   396→                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n   397→                    windowState.clear();\n   398→                }\n   399→                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n   400→            }\n   401→\n   402→            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n   403→            mergingWindows.persist();\n   404→        } else {\n   405→            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n   406→\n   407→                // drop if the window is already late\n   408→                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n   409→                    continue;\n   410→                }\n   411→                isSkippedElement = false;\n   412→\n   413→                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n   414→                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n   415→\n   416→                triggerContext.key = key;\n   417→                triggerContext.window = window;\n   418→\n   419→                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n   420→\n   421→                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n   422→                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n   423→                    if (contents != null) {\n   424→                        emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n   425→                    }\n   426→                }\n   427→\n   428→                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n   429→                    windowState.clear();\n   430→                }\n   431→                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n   432→            }\n   433→        }\n   434→\n   435→        // side output input event if\n   436→        // element not handled by any window\n   437→        // late arriving tag has been set\n   438→        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n   439→        // element timestamp\n   440→        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n   441→            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n   442→                sideOutput(element);\n   443→            } else {\n   444→                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n   445→            }\n   446→        }\n   447→    }\n   448→\n   449→    @Override\n   450→    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n   451→        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n   452→        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n   453→\n   454→        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n   455→\n   456→        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n   457→            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n   458→            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n   459→            if (stateWindow == null) {\n   460→                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n   461→                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n   462→                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n   463→                return;\n   464→            } else {\n   465→                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n   466→            }\n   467→        } else {\n   468→            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n   469→            mergingWindows = null;\n   470→        }\n   471→\n   472→        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n   473→\n   474→        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n   475→            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n   476→            if (contents != null) {\n   477→                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n   478→            }\n   479→        }\n   480→\n   481→        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n   482→            windowState.clear();\n   483→        }\n   484→\n   485→        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n   486→                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n   487→            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n   488→        }\n   489→\n   490→        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n   491→            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n   492→            mergingWindows.persist();\n   493→        }\n   494→    }\n   495→\n   496→    @Override\n   497→    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n   498→        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n   499→        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n   500→\n   501→        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n   502→\n   503→        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n   504→            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n   505→            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n   506→            if (stateWindow == null) {\n   507→                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n   508→                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n   509→                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n   510→                return;\n   511→            } else {\n   512→                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n   513→            }\n   514→        } else {\n   515→            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n   516→            mergingWindows = null;\n   517→        }\n   518→\n   519→        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n   520→\n   521→        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n   522→            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n   523→            if (contents != null) {\n   524→                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n   525→            }\n   526→        }\n   527→\n   528→        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n   529→            windowState.clear();\n   530→        }\n   531→\n   532→        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n   533→                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n   534→            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n   535→        }\n   536→\n   537→        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n   538→            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n   539→            mergingWindows.persist();\n   540→        }\n   541→    }\n   542→\n   543→    @Override\n   544→    public OperatorAttributes getOperatorAttributes() {\n   545→        boolean return new OperatorAttributesBuilder()\n   549→                .setOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream(isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream)\n   550→                .build();\n   551→    }\n   552→\n   553→    /**\n   554→     * Drops all state for the given window and calls {@link Trigger#clear(Window,\n   555→     * Trigger.TriggerContext)}.\n   556→     *\n   557→     * <p>The caller must ensure that the correct key is set in the state backend and the\n   558→     * triggerContext object.\n   559→     */\n   560→    private void clearAllState(\n   561→            W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n   562→            throws Exception {\n   563→        windowState.clear();\n   564→        triggerContext.clear();\n   565→        processContext.window = window;\n   566→        processContext.clear();\n   567→        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n   568→            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n   569→            mergingWindows.persist();\n   570→        }\n   571→    }\n   572→\n   573→    /** Emits the contents of the given window using the {@link InternalWindowFunction}. */\n   574→    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   575→    private void emitWindowContents(W window, ACC contents) throws Exception {\n   576→        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n   577→        processContext.window = window;\n   578→        userFunction.process(\n   579→                triggerContext.key, window, processContext, contents, timestampedCollector);\n   580→    }\n   581→\n   582→    /**\n   583→     * Write skipped late arriving element to SideOutput.\n   584→     *\n   585→     * @param element skipped late arriving element to side output\n   586→     */\n   587→    protected void sideOutput(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n   588→        output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element);\n   589→    }\n   590→\n   591→    /**\n   592→     * Retrieves the {@link MergingWindowSet} for the currently active key. The caller must ensure\n   593→     * that the correct key is set in the state backend.\n   594→     *\n   595→     * <p>The caller must also ensure to properly persist changes to state using {@link\n   596→     * MergingWindowSet#persist()}.\n   597→     */\n   598→    protected MergingWindowSet<W> getMergingWindowSet() throws Exception {\n   599→        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n   600→        MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W> mergingAssigner =\n   601→                (MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W>) windowAssigner;\n   602→        return new MergingWindowSet<>(mergingAssigner, mergingSetsState);\n   603→    }\n   604→\n   605→    /**\n   606→     * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\n   607→     * the given window.\n   608→     */\n   609→    protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\n   610→        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n   611→                && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n   612→    }\n   613→\n   614→    /**\n   615→     * Decide if a record is currently late, based on current watermark and allowed lateness.\n   616→     *\n   617→     * @param element The element to check\n   618→     * @return The element for which should be considered when sideoutputs\n   619→     */\n   620→    protected boolean isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n   621→        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime())\n   622→                && (element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n   623→                        <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark());\n   624→    }\n   625→\n   626→    /**\n   627→     * Registers a timer to cleanup the content of the window.\n   628→     *\n   629→     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n   630→     */\n   631→    protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\n   632→        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n   633→        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n   634→            // don't set a GC timer for \"end of time\"\n   635→            return;\n   636→        }\n   637→\n   638→        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n   639→            triggerContext.registerEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n   640→        } else {\n   641→            triggerContext.registerProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n   642→        }\n   643→    }\n   644→\n   645→    /**\n   646→     * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\n   647→     *\n   648→     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n   649→     */\n   650→    protected void deleteCleanupTimer(W window) {\n   651→        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n   652→        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n   653→            // no need to clean up because we didn't set one\n   654→            return;\n   655→        }\n   656→        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n   657→            triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n   658→        } else {\n   659→            triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n   660→        }\n   661→    }\n   662→\n   663→    /**\n   664→     * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp +\n   665→     * allowedLateness}. In case this leads to a value greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} then a\n   666→     * cleanup time of {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} is returned.\n   667→     *\n   668→     * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\n   669→     */\n   670→    private long cleanupTime(W window) {\n   671→        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n   672→            long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\n   673→            return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\n   674→        } else {\n   675→            return window.maxTimestamp();\n   676→        }\n   677→    }\n   678→\n   679→    /** Returns {@code true} if the given time is the cleanup time for the given window. */\n   680→    protected final boolean isCleanupTime(W window, long time) {\n   681→        return time == cleanupTime(window);\n   682→    }\n   683→\n   684→    /**\n   685→     * Base class for per-window {@link KeyedStateStore KeyedStateStores}. java.util.Collection;\n\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n\n/**\n * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n * Trigger}.\n *\n * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n * WindowAssigner}.\n *\n * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n        implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Configuration values and user functions\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n\n    private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n\n    private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n\n    /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n\n    /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n\n    /**\n     * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n     *\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n     *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n     *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n     * </ul>\n     */\n    protected final long allowedLateness;\n\n    /**\n     * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n     * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n     * to this.\n     */\n    protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n\n    private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n\n    protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that is not checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n    private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n\n    /**\n     * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n     * state is not mergeable.\n     */\n    private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n\n    /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n    private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n\n    /**\n     * This is given to the {@code InternalWindowFunction} for emitting elements with a given\n     * timestamp.\n     */\n    protected transient TimestampedCollector<OUT> timestampedCollector;\n\n    protected transient Context triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n\n    protected transient WindowContext processContext;\n\n    protected transient WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext windowAssignerContext;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that needs to be checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n\n    /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n    public WindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n        super(windowFunction);\n\n        checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n\n        checkArgument(\n                windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n                \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n\n        this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n        this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n        this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\n        this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\n        this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\n        this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\n        this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\n        this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setup(\n            StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\n            StreamConfig config,\n            Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\n        super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\n        super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n        timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n\n        internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\"window-timers\", windowSerializer, this);\n\n        triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n        processContext = new WindowContext(null);\n\n        windowAssignerContext =\n                new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                    @Override\n                    public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                        return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                    }\n                };\n\n        // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\n        // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\n        if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\n            windowState =\n                    (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\n        }\n\n        // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n\n            // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\n            if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n                windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>) windowState;\n            } else if (windowState != null) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\n                        \"The window uses a merging assigner, but the window state is not mergeable.\");\n            }\n\n            @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n            final Class<Tuple2<W, W>> typedTuple = (Class<Tuple2<W, W>>) (Class<?>) Tuple2.class;\n\n            final TupleSerializer<Tuple2<W, W>> tupleSerializer =\n                    new TupleSerializer<>(\n                            typedTuple, new TypeSerializer[] {windowSerializer, windowSerializer});\n\n            final ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsStateDescriptor =\n                    new ListStateDescriptor<>(\"merging-window-set\", tupleSerializer);\n\n            // get the state that stores the merging sets\n            mergingSetsState =\n                    (InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(\n                                    VoidNamespaceSerializer.INSTANCE, mergingSetsStateDescriptor);\n            mergingSetsState.setCurrentNamespace(VoidNamespace.INSTANCE);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        timestampedCollector = null;\n        triggerContext = null;\n        processContext = null;\n        windowAssignerContext = null;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n\n        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n\n        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                // actualWindow == window\n                W actualWindow =\n                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                window,\n                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                    @Override\n                                    public void merge(\n                                            W mergeResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                            W stateWindowResult,\n                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                            throws Exception {\n\n                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                        <= internalTimerService\n                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                            + internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n                                                            + \" window: \"\n                                                            + mergeResult);\n                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n                                            long currentProcessingTime =\n                                                    internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                                            if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                    <= currentProcessingTime) {\n                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                                + currentProcessingTime\n                                                                + \" window: \"\n                                                                + mergeResult);\n                                            }\n                                        }\n\n                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                        triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                        for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                            triggerContext.window = m;\n                                            triggerContext.clear();\n                                            deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                        }\n\n                                        // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                        // state window\n                                        windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                    }\n                                });\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                    mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n                if (stateWindow == null) {\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\n                            \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n                }\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n            }\n\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        } else {\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = window;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // side output input event if\n        // element not handled by any window\n        // late arriving tag has been set\n        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n        // element timestamp\n        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n                sideOutput(element);\n            } else {\n                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public OperatorAttributes getOperatorAttributes() {\n        boolean isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream =\n                windowAssigner instanceof GlobalWindows\n                        && trigger instanceof GlobalWindows.EndOfStreamTrigger;\n        return new OperatorAttributesBuilder()\n                .setOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream(isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream)\n                .build();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Drops all state for the given window and calls {@link Trigger#clear(Window,\n     * Trigger.TriggerContext)}.\n     *\n     * <p>The caller must ensure that the correct key is set in the state backend and the\n     * triggerContext object.\n     */\n    private void clearAllState(\n            W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n            throws Exception {\n        windowState.clear();\n        triggerContext.clear();\n        processContext.window = window;\n        processContext.clear();\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Emits the contents of the given window using the {@link InternalWindowFunction}. */\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private void emitWindowContents(W window, ACC contents) throws Exception {\n        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n        processContext.window = window;\n        userFunction.process(\n                triggerContext.key, window, processContext, contents, timestampedCollector);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write skipped late arriving element to SideOutput.\n     *\n     * @param element skipped late arriving element to side output\n     */\n    protected void sideOutput(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n        output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Retrieves the {@link MergingWindowSet} for the currently active key. The caller must ensure\n     * that the correct key is set in the state backend.\n     *\n     * <p>The caller must also ensure to properly persist changes to state using {@link\n     * MergingWindowSet#persist()}.\n     */\n    protected MergingWindowSet<W> getMergingWindowSet() throws Exception {\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W> mergingAssigner =\n                (MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W>) windowAssigner;\n        return new MergingWindowSet<>(mergingAssigner, mergingSetsState);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\n     * the given window.\n     */\n    protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\n        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Decide if a record is currently late, based on current watermark and allowed lateness.\n     *\n     * @param element The element to check\n     * @return The element for which should be considered when sideoutputs\n     */\n    protected boolean isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime())\n                && (element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                        <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark());\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Registers a timer to cleanup the content of the window.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n     */\n    protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\n        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n            // don't set a GC timer for \"end of time\"\n            return;\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            triggerContext.registerEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        } else {\n            triggerContext.registerProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n     */\n    protected void deleteCleanupTimer(W window) {\n        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n            // no need to clean up because we didn't set one\n            return;\n        }\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        } else {\n            triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp +\n     * allowedLateness}. In case this leads to a value greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} then a\n     * cleanup time of {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} is returned.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\n     */\n    private long cleanupTime(W window) {\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\n            return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\n        } else {\n            return window.maxTimestamp();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Returns {@code true} if the given time is the cleanup time for the given window. */\n    protected final boolean isCleanupTime(W window, long time) {\n        return time == cleanupTime(window);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Base class for per-window {@link KeyedStateStore KeyedStateStores}. Used to allow per-window\n     * state access for {@link\n     * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\n     */\n    public abstract class AbstractPerWindowStateStore extends DefaultKeyedStateStore {\n\n        // we have this in the base class even though it's not used in MergingKeyStore so that\n        // we can always set it and ignore what actual implementation we have\n        protected W window;\n\n        public AbstractPerWindowStateStore(\n                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n            super(\n                    keyedStateBackend,\n                    new SerializerFactory() {\n                        @Override\n                        public <T> TypeSerializer<T> createSerializer(\n                                TypeInformation<T> typeInformation) {\n                            return typeInformation.createSerializer(\n                                    executionConfig.getSerializerConfig());\n                        }\n                    });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Special {@link AbstractPerWindowStateStore} that doesn't allow access to per-window state.\n     */\n    public class MergingWindowStateStore extends AbstractPerWindowStateStore {\n\n        public MergingWindowStateStore(\n                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n            super(keyedStateBackend, executionConfig);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <T> ValueState<T> getState(ValueStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <T> ListState<T> getListState(ListStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <T> ReducingState<T> getReducingState(ReducingStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <IN, ACC, OUT> AggregatingState<IN, OUT> getAggregatingState(\n                AggregatingStateDescriptor<IN, ACC, OUT> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <UK, UV> MapState<UK, UV> getMapState(MapStateDescriptor<UK, UV> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Regular per-window state store for use with {@link\n     * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\n     */\n    public class PerWindowStateStore extends AbstractPerWindowStateStore {\n\n        public PerWindowStateStore(\n                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n            super(keyedStateBackend, executionConfig);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        protected <S extends State> S getPartitionedState(StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor)\n                throws Exception {\n            return keyedStateBackend.getPartitionedState(window, windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * A utility class for handling {@code ProcessWindowFunction} invocations. This can be reused by\n     * setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept in the\n     * {@code WindowContext}.\n     */\n    public class WindowContext implements InternalWindowFunction.InternalWindowContext {\n        protected W window;\n\n        protected AbstractPerWindowStateStore windowState;\n\n        public WindowContext(W window) {\n            this.window = window;\n            this.windowState =\n                    windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner\n                            ? new MergingWindowStateStore(\n                                    getKeyedStateBackend(), getExecutionConfig())\n                            : new PerWindowStateStore(getKeyedStateBackend(), getExecutionConfig());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"WindowContext{Window = \" + window.toString() + \"}\";\n        }\n\n        public void clear() throws Exception {\n            userFunction.clear(window, this);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long currentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long currentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public KeyedStateStore windowState() {\n            this.windowState.window = this.window;\n            return this.windowState;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public KeyedStateStore globalState() {\n            return WindowOperator.this.getKeyedStateStore();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <X> void output(OutputTag<X> outputTag, X value) {\n            if (outputTag == null) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"OutputTag must not be null.\");\n            }\n            output.collect(outputTag, new StreamRecord<>(value, window.maxTimestamp()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * {@code Context} is a utility for handling {@code Trigger} invocations. It can be reused by\n     * setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept in the\n     * {@code Context}\n     */\n    public class Context implements Trigger.OnMergeContext {\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        protected Collection<W> mergedWindows;\n\n        public Context(K key, W window) {\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        public <S extends State> S getPartitionedState(StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor) {\n            try {\n                return WindowOperator.this.getPartitionedState(\n                        window, windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n            } catch (Exception e) {\n                throw new RuntimeException(\"Could not retrieve state\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <S extends MergingState<?, ?>> void mergePartitionedState(\n                StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor) {\n            if (mergedWindows != null && mergedWindows.size() > 0) {\n                try {\n                    S rawState =\n                            getKeyedStateBackend()\n                                    .getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n\n                    if (rawState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n                        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n                        InternalMergingState<K, W, ?, ?, ?> mergingState =\n                                (InternalMergingState<K, W, ?, ?, ?>) rawState;\n                        mergingState.mergeNamespaces(window, mergedWindows);\n                    } else {\n                        throw new IllegalArgumentException(\n                                \"The given state descriptor does not refer to a mergeable state (MergingState)\");\n                    }\n                } catch (Exception e) {\n                    throw new RuntimeException(\"Error while merging state.\", e);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void registerProcessingTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.registerProcessingTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void registerEventTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.registerEventTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void deleteProcessingTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void deleteEventTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.deleteEventTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        public TriggerResult onElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n            return trigger.onElement(element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), window, this);\n        }\n\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time) throws Exception {\n            return trigger.onProcessingTime(time, window, this);\n        }\n\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time) throws Exception {\n            return trigger.onEventTime(time, window, this);\n        }\n\n        public void onMerge(Collection<W> mergedWindows) throws Exception {\n            this.mergedWindows = mergedWindows;\n            trigger.onMerge(window, this);\n        }\n\n        public void clear() throws Exception {\n            trigger.clear(window, this);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"Context{\" + \"key=\" + key + \", window=\" + window + '}';\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Internal class for keeping track of in-flight timers. */\n    protected static class Timer<K, W extends Window> implements Comparable<Timer<K, W>> {\n        protected long timestamp;\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        public Timer(long timestamp, K key, W window) {\n            this.timestamp = timestamp;\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public int compareTo(Timer<K, W> o) {\n            return Long.compare(this.timestamp, o.timestamp);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean equals(Object o) {\n            if (this == o) {\n                return true;\n            }\n            if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass()) {\n                return false;\n            }\n\n            Timer<?, ?> timer = (Timer<?, ?>) o;\n\n            return timestamp == timer.timestamp\n                    && key.equals(timer.key)\n                    && window.equals(timer.window);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public int hashCode() {\n            int {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_011EZcgDCL6KhehPbRiy9SEU","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Now I can see the issue. Looking at the `processElement()` method (starting at line 293), I can see the bug:\n\nFor merging windows (line 303), the code:\n1. Iterates through assigned windows\n2. Calls `mergingWindows.addWindow()` which can cause merges\n3. Then checks `if (isWindowLate(actualWindow))` at line 369\n\nBut the problem is more subtle. Let me trace through the code:\n\nLooking at lines 440-446:\n```java\n// side output input event if\n// element not handled by any window\n// late arriving tag has been set\n// windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n// element timestamp\nif (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n    if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n        sideOutput(element);\n    } else {\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThe issue is:\n1. For merging windows, when an element arrives:\n   - It gets assigned to windows\n   - For each window, it tries to add it to the merging window set\n   - After adding (which can merge existing windows), it checks `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` - if true, it retires the window and continues (skips processing)\n   - If the window is not late, it adds the element to the window state\n\n2. The problem: An element can arrive AFTER the watermark (isElementLate returns true), but it could still merge with an existing session window that hasn't been cleaned up yet. \n\n3. The current code:\n   - For merging windows: checks if the *window* is late AFTER merging, but doesn't emit to side output when skipping\n   - Then at the end, if `isSkippedElement` is true AND `isElementLate` is true, it emits to side output\n   - But `isSkippedElement` gets set to false if ANY window accepts the element\n   \nWhen a late element could have merged with an existing window that's subsequently marked as late and retired, the element gets silently dropped without being sent to the side output. The merging windows path (lines 303-403) skips the element when the window is late but never sets `isSkippedElement` to true, so the final late-element check never triggers.\n\nThe core issue is that session windows can extend existing sessions when elements fall within the gap distance, but if that session has already been retired due to lateness, the element is lost entirely rather than being routed to the side output as expected.\n\nWhen an element arrives with timestamp T and the watermark W means T + allowedLateness <= W, the element is marked late. However, if that element could have merged with an active session window, the merged result might have a maxTimestamp that keeps it within the allowed lateness window. The problem occurs when the session has already been retired—the merge logic doesn't account for this scenario, so the element gets dropped instead of being properly handled as a late arrival. Looking at the code flow more carefully, when an element arrives with timestamp T and `isElementLate(element)` returns true, the code still attempts to add it to the window set for merging windows. If the resulting window is also late (`isWindowLate(actualWindow)` is true), it gets skipped while `isSkippedElement` remains true. Then at the end of `processElement()`, the condition `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))` triggers the side output emission, but only if `lateDataOutputTag` is not null. I need to compare this against the non-merging case to understand where the actual problem lies.\n\nIn the non-merging path, when a window is determined to be late, it's skipped without side output, leaving `isSkippedElement` as true. The same final check then emits to side output if the element itself is late. The `sideOutput()` method itself just delegates to `output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element)`, so the issue must be elsewhere in how the output object handles this call.\n\nRe-reading the problem description more carefully, the core issue appears to be that when `sideOutput()` is invoked, the output collector doesn't have the late data tag properly registered, causing the element to be silently discarded rather than routed to the side output.\n\nLooking at the merging windows logic, I suspect the problem lies in the sequence of checks. The current implementation validates whether a window is late after the merge completes, then skips processing. Later, when attempting to emit to the side output, the collector may lack the necessary tag registration. The test cases should clarify what behavior we're actually trying to achieve here.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":12,"cache_creation_input_tokens":13630,"cache_read_input_tokens":22431,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":13630},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f3eab87a-9d2f-419a-a643-4f9ae6cfb7cd","uuid":"e6091cbb-3239-4d81-8784-6c539bb38cef"}

The problem is specific to merging windows:\n\nFor a session window with merging:\n1. Element arrives at timestamp T with watermark W\n\n\n2. `elementWindows = windowAssigner.assignWindows(element)` might return an empty collection if the element is too late to form a new window but could merge with existing ones\n3. If elementWindows is empty, the loop doesn't execute\n4. `isSkippedElement` remains true\n5. At the end, if `isElementLate(element)` is true, we emit to side output\n\nBut there's a more subtle issue with session windows specifically. The WindowAssigner assigns elements to windows based on the gap duration, so when an element arrives late, it might not get assigned to any window at all if it falls outside the allowed lateness window.\n\nHowever, for session windows, an element that extends an existing window might not actually be \"assigned\" a new window - it just gets merged with the existing one. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01EcPKRLnQsSFf4sUgqcerjm","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Aha! Now I see. `EventTimeSessionWindows.assignWindows()` always returns a single window: `new TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp + sessionTimeout)`.\n\nSo when an element arrives, even if it's late, it will always be assigned a window (or rather, ONE window). Then in the merging process, that window might be merged with existing windows.\n\nNow let me trace through what happens:\n\n1. Late element arrives at timestamp T with watermark W, where T + allowedLateness <= W\n2. `elementWindows = windowAssigner.assignWindows(element)` returns one window: `[TimeWindow(T, T + sessionTimeout)]`\n3. In the merging loop (line 306):\n   - We call `mergingWindows.addWindow(TimeWindow(T, T + sessionTimeout), mergeFunction)`\n   - This might merge with existing windows, returning an `actualWindow`\n   - We check `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` - is `actualWindow.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= W`?\n4. If `actualWindow.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness > W`, then the window is NOT late, and we process the element normally\n5. If `actualWindow.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= W`, then the window IS late, we retire it and continue\n6. At the end, if `isSkippedElement` is true and `isElementLate(element)` is true, we emit to side output\n\nSo the logic seems correct to me so far. Let me think about the actual bug...\n\nWait, maybe the bug is this: an element arrives LATE (T + allowedLateness <= W), and gets assigned to a NEW window that doesn't exist yet. The window will be late (because T + allowedLateness <= W implies the newly created window with max timestamp T + sessionTimeout will also be late). So we skip it without adding it to state.\n\nThen at the end, we check `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))` and emit to side output. \n\nBut what if the late element could have extended an existing session that's still active? The merging logic might be the culprit here—I need to understand how `MergingWindowSet` actually combines windows to see if there's a gap in the logic.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1763,"cache_read_input_tokens":43441,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1763},"output_tokens":5,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f3eab87a-9d2f-419a-a643-4f9ae6cfb7cd","uuid":"8158fb3b-9402-4b63-9959-616fd75d1130"}

Elements from the state windows of merged\n    42→ * windows must be merged into this one state window. We keep a mapping from in-flight window to\n    43→ * state window that can be queried using {@link #getStateWindow(Window)}.\n    44→ *\n    45→ * <p>A new window can be added to the set of in-flight windows using {@link #addWindow(Window,\n    46→ * MergeFunction)}. This might merge other windows and the caller must react accordingly in the\n    47→ * {@link MergeFunction#merge(Object, Collection, Object, Collection)} and adjust the outside view\n    48→ * of windows and state.\n    49→ *\n    50→ * <p>Windows can be removed from the set of windows using {@link #retireWindow(Window)}.\n    51→ *\n    52→ * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that this set is keeping track of.\n    53→ */\n    54→public class MergingWindowSet<W extends Window> {\n    55→\n    56→    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MergingWindowSet.class);\n    57→\n    58→    /**\n    59→     * Mapping from window to the window that keeps the window state. When we are incrementally\n    60→     * merging windows starting from some window we keep that starting window as the state window to\n    61→     * prevent costly state juggling.\n    62→     */\n    63→    private final Map<W, W> mapping;\n    64→\n    65→    /**\n    66→     * Mapping when we created the {@code MergingWindowSet}. The state window is the\n   110→     * {@code Window} in which we keep the actual state of a given in-flight window. Windows might\n   111→     * expand but we keep to original state window for keeping the elements of the window to avoid\n   112→     * costly state juggling.\n   113→     *\n   114→     * @param window The window for which to get the state window.\n   115→     */\n   116→    public W getStateWindow(W window) {\n   117→        return mapping.get(window);\n   118→    }\n   119→\n   120→    /**\n   121→     * Removes the given window from the set of in-flight windows.\n   122→     *\n   123→     * @param window The {@code Window} to remove.\n   124→     */\n   125→    public void retireWindow(W window) {\n   126→        W removed = this.mapping.remove(window);\n   127→        if (removed == null) {\n   128→            throw new IllegalStateException(\n   129→                    \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n   130→        }\n   131→    }\n   132→\n   133→    /**\n   134→     * Adds a new {@code Window} to the set of in-flight windows. It might happen that this triggers\n   135→     * merging of previously in-flight windows. In that case, the provided {@link MergeFunction} is\n   136→     * called.\n   137→     *\n   138→     * <p>This returns the window that is the representative of the added window after adding. This\n   139→     * can either be the new window itself, if no merge occurred, or the newly merged window. Adding\n   140→     * an element to a window or calling trigger functions should only happen on the returned\n   141→     * representative. This way, we never have to deal with a new window that is immediately\n   142→     * swallowed up by another window.\n   143→     *\n   144→     * <p>If the new window is merged, the {@code MergeFunction} callback arguments also don't\n   145→     * contain the new window as part of the list of merged windows.\n   146→     *\n   147→     * @param newWindow The new {@code Window} to add.\n   148→     * @param mergeFunction The callback to be invoked in case a merge occurs.\n   149→     * @return The {@code Window} that new new {@code Window} ended up in. This can also be the new\n   150→     *     {@code Window} itself in case no merge occurred.\n   151→     * @throws Exception\n   152→     */\n   153→    public W addWindow(W newWindow, mergedWindows,\n   213→                        this.mapping.get(mergeResult),\n   214→                        mergedStateWindows);\n   215→            }\n   216→        }\n   217→\n   218→        //
