Elements from the state windows of merged\n * windows must be merged into this one state window. We keep a mapping from in-flight window to\n * state window that can be queried using {@link #getStateWindow(Window)}.\n *\n * <p>A new window can be added to the set of in-flight windows using {@link #addWindow(Window,\n * MergeFunction)}. This might merge other windows and the caller must react accordingly in the\n * {@link MergeFunction#merge(Object, Collection, Object, Collection)} and adjust the outside view\n * of windows and state.\n *\n * <p>Windows can be removed from the set of windows using {@link #retireWindow(Window)}.\n *\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that this set is keeping track of.\n */\npublic class MergingWindowSet<W extends Window> {\n\n    private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MergingWindowSet.class);\n\n    /**\n     * Mapping from window to the window that keeps the window state. When we are incrementally\n     * merging windows starting from some window we keep that starting window as the state window to\n     * prevent costly state juggling.\n     */\n    private final Map<W, W> mapping;\n\n    /**\n     * Mapping when we created the {@code MergingWindowSet}. We use this to decide whether we need\n     * to persist any changes to state.\n     */\n    private final Map<W, W> initialMapping;\n\n    private final ListState<Tuple2<W, W>> state;\n\n    /** Our window assigner. */\n    private final MergingWindowAssigner<?, W> windowAssigner;\n\n    /** Restores a {@link MergingWindowSet} from the given state. */\n    public MergingWindowSet(\n            MergingWindowAssigner<?, W> windowAssigner, ListState<Tuple2<W, W>> state)\n            throws Exception {\n        this.windowAssigner = windowAssigner;\n        mapping = new HashMap<>();\n\n        Iterable<Tuple2<W, W>> windowState = state.get();\n        if (windowState != null) {\n            for (Tuple2<W, W> window : windowState) {\n                mapping.put(window.f0, window.f1);\n            }\n        }\n\n        this.state = state;\n\n        initialMapping = new HashMap<>();\n        initialMapping.putAll(mapping);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Persist the updated mapping to the given state if the mapping changed since initialization.\n     */\n    public void persist() throws Exception {\n        if (!mapping.equals(initialMapping)) {\n            state.update(\n                    mapping.entrySet().stream()\n                            .map((w) -> new Tuple2<>(w.getKey(), w.getValue()))\n                            .collect(Collectors.toList()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the state window for the given in-flight {@code Window}. The state window is the\n     * {@code Window} in which we keep the actual state of a given in-flight window. Windows might\n     * expand but we keep to original state window for keeping the elements of the window to avoid\n     * costly state juggling.\n     *\n     * @param window The window for which to get the state window.\n     */\n    public W getStateWindow(W window) {\n        return mapping.get(window);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Removes the given window from the set of in-flight windows.\n     *\n     * @param window The {@code Window} to remove.\n     */\n    public void retireWindow(W window) {\n        W removed = this.mapping.remove(window);\n        if (removed == null) {\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\n                    \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Adds a new {@code Window} to the set of in-flight windows. It might happen that this triggers\n     * merging of previously in-flight windows. In that case, the provided {@link MergeFunction} is\n     * called.\n     *\n     * <p>This returns the window that is the representative of the added window after adding. This\n     * can either be the new window itself, if no merge occurred, or the newly merged window. Adding\n     * an element to a window or calling trigger functions should only happen on the returned\n     * representative. This way, we never have to deal with a new window that is immediately\n     * swallowed up by another window.\n     *\n     * <p>If the new window is merged, the {@code MergeFunction} callback arguments also don't\n     * contain the new window as part of the list of merged windows.\n     *\n     * @param newWindow The new {@code Window} to add.\n     * @param mergeFunction The callback to be invoked in case a merge occurs.\n     * @return The {@code Window} that new new {@code Window} ended up in. This can also be the new\n     *     {@code Window} itself in case no merge occurred.\n     * @throws Exception\n     */\n    public W addWindow(W newWindow, MergeFunction<W> mergeFunction) throws Exception {\n\n        List<W> windows = new ArrayList<>();\n\n        windows.addAll(this.mapping.keySet());\n        windows.add(newWindow);\n\n        final Map<W, Collection<W>> mergeResults = new HashMap<>();\n        windowAssigner.mergeWindows(\n                windows,\n                new MergingWindowAssigner.MergeCallback<W>() {\n                    @Override\n                    public void merge(Collection<W> toBeMerged, W mergeResult) {\n                        if (LOG.isDebugEnabled()) {\n                            LOG.debug(\"Merging {} into {}\", toBeMerged, mergeResult);\n                        }\n                        mergeResults.put(mergeResult, toBeMerged);\n                    }\n                });\n\n        W resultWindow = newWindow;\n        boolean mergedNewWindow = false;\n\n        // perform the merge\n        for (Map.Entry<W, Collection<W>> c : mergeResults.entrySet()) {\n            W mergeResult = c.getKey();\n            Collection<W> mergedWindows = c.getValue();\n\n            // if our new window is in the merged windows make the merge result the\n            // result window\n            if (mergedWindows.remove(newWindow)) {\n                mergedNewWindow = true;\n                resultWindow = mergeResult;\n            }\n\n            // pick any of the merged windows and choose that window's state window\n            // as the state window for the merge result\n            W mergedStateWindow = this.mapping.get(mergedWindows.iterator().next());\n\n            // figure out the state windows that we are merging\n            List<W> mergedStateWindows = new ArrayList<>();\n            for (W mergedWindow : mergedWindows) {\n                W res = this.mapping.remove(mergedWindow);\n                if (res != null) {\n                    mergedStateWindows.add(res);\n                }\n            }\n\n            this.mapping.put(mergeResult, mergedStateWindow);\n\n            // don't put the target state window into the merged windows\n            mergedStateWindows.remove(mergedStateWindow);\n\n            // don't merge the new window itself, it never had any state associated with it\n            // i.e. if we are only merging one pre-existing window into itself\n            // without extending the pre-existing window\n            if (!(mergedWindows.contains(mergeResult) && mergedWindows.size() == 1)) {\n                mergeFunction.merge(\n                        mergeResult,\n                        mergedWindows,\n                        this.mapping.get(mergeResult),\n                        mergedStateWindows);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // the new window created a new, self-contained window without merging\n        if (mergeResults.isEmpty() || (resultWindow.equals(newWindow) && !mergedNewWindow)) {\n            this.mapping.put(resultWindow, resultWindow);\n        }\n\n        return resultWindow;\n    }\n\n merge occurs.\n         *\n         * @param mergeResult The newly resulting merged {@code Window}.\n         * @param mergedWindows The merged {@code Window Windows}.\n         * @param stateWindowResult The state window of the merge result.\n         * @param mergedStateWindows The merged state windows.\n         * @throws Exception\n         */\n        void merge(\n For a session window, this element gets assigned to a window: `[TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp + gap)]`\n3. This new window is then merged with existing windows using `mergingWindows.addWindow()`\n4. If the merged window is also late (mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= watermark), it's skipped\n5. BUT - the element is never emitted to the side output in the normal late data path!\n\nActually wait, let me re-read the code one more time...\n\nLooking at lines 440-446 in WindowOperator.processElement():\n```java\nif (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n    if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n        sideOutput(element);\n    } else {\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n    }\n}```\n\nThis checks:\n- `isSkippedElement` - element not handled by any window (true if all windows were skipped)\n- `isElementLate(element)` - timestamp + allowedLateness <= watermark\n\nIf both are true and lateDataOutputTag is set, it calls `sideOutput(element)`.\n\nSo the question is: when would `isSkippedElement` be true but `isElementLate(element)` be false? And vice versa?\n\nActually, the condition should probably be `isSkippedElement` (regardless of whether it's late). If an element is skipped, it should be emitted to the side output if the tag is set, unless... unless the window is late but the element itself is not considered late?\n\nActually, I think I see the issue now! The bug is that:\n- For non-merging windows, if a window is late, we skip it (continue), and `isSkippedElement` remains true\n\n\n- For merging windows, when an element gets assigned to a new window that then merges with existing ones, the resulting merged window might extend beyond the late threshold. In this case, `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` could be true while `isElementLate(element)` is false, since the element's timestamp itself might still be within the allowed lateness window even though the merged window extends past it.\n\nActually, let me reconsider - if `isWindowLate(actualWindow)` is true, meaning `actualWindow.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= watermark`, and the element was added to this window, then `element.timestamp <= actualWindow.maxTimestamp()`. This means `element.timestamp + allowedLateness <= actualWindow.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness <= watermark`, so the element should also be considered late. The key insight is that an element's lateness depends on its own timestamp relative to the watermark, not the window's end time.\n\nGiven this logic, `isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)` should reduce to just `isSkippedElement` since any element in a late window must itself be late. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01MdN9y3ujtikLtVusLXWwwY","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"I don't have permission to install packages. Let me just analyze the code directly to understand the bug. Based on my reading of the code and the existing tests, I think I can now identify what the issue might be.\n\nLet me re-examine the code path for merging windows. When an element arrives:1. It gets assigned to windows by the window assigner\n2. For each window, we call `mergingWindows.addWindow()` which might merge with existing windows\n3. We check `isWindowLate(actualWindow)`\n4. If late, we retire the window and skip\n5. At the end, if all windows were skipped and the element is late, we emit to side output\n\nThe key question is: what happens if the element merges with an existing window but the merged window is marked as late and retired?\n\nIn that case:- The window was in the merging window set before\n- We add the new element, which merges it with an existing window\n- The merged window is marked as late and retired\n- But the element state is never added to the window because we skipped it\n\nNow, if an element could have extended a session window (by merging), but we mark it as late and retire it before adding the element, then the element is lost.\n\nHowever, looking at the MergingWindowSet.addWindow() code:1. It collects all in-flight windows + the new window\n2. Calls mergeWindows() to determine merge results\n3. For each merge:   - If new window is in the merged windows, it becomes part of the merged result\n   - Otherwise, it creates a new window entry\n\nSo when we add an element that merges with an existing window, the newWindow is added to the list of windows passed to mergeWindows(). If it merges, the mergeResult becomes the new representative.\n\nThen back in WindowOperator.processElement():- We get the actualWindow (which might be a merged window)\n- We check isWindowLate(actualWindow)\n- If late, we retire it and skip\n\n\n- But wait, the task description says lateness is checked BEFORE merging, yet the code checks it AFTER. This discrepancy suggests the current implementation might be correct, or perhaps the description is highlighting what should happen versus what's actually happening. Let me trace through the actual execution flow more carefully to determine if there's truly a bug here.\n\nThe task clarifies the real requirement: before marking an element as late, I need to first check whether it could merge into an existing, non-expired window. Only if no such window exists should the element be classified as late. This means reordering the logic so the merge compatibility check happens before the lateness evaluation, allowing valid merges to proceed even if the element arrives after the watermark. window, and this extended window is late (maxTimestamp + allowedLateness <= watermark), then we skip it.\n\nBut the element might still be valid if the previous window wasn't late before the merge. When a session window extends an existing one, it creates a new merged window with an updated maxTimestamp. The real problem is that we're checking lateness on the merged result without considering whether the element itself should have been accepted into the original window before the merge happened.\n\nI see it now—the bug occurs when an element arrives after the watermark but gets assigned to a window that merges with an earlier non-late window. The merged result becomes late, so we discard the element entirely. However, if the original window (before merging) wasn't late, the element should have been processed. The lateness check happens after merging, which means we're rejecting elements that should have been valid based on their original window assignment.\n\nWhen we call `mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow)` on a late merged window without adding the element, we're removing a window that might have contained valid data. The retirement itself might be problematic if it affects the state of windows that should still be active. The core issue is the order of operations: we merge first, check lateness on the merged window, and only then decide whether to process the element—but by that point, we've already committed to the merged window's lateness status. the window from the merging set. If a window was just created through merging from an existing one, retiring it could lead to state loss for the previously merged windows. However, looking at the code, the state window is tracked separately from the actual window, so retiring the actual window shouldn't affect the state. I should examine a concrete test case to understand what's actually failing. Let me trace through a specific scenario: an element arrives at T=1000 creating window [1000, 1003], then the watermark advances to 1001.\n\nWindow [1000, 1003] isn't late since 1003 + 0 > 1001. At T=1002, a late element with timestamp 999 arrives, creating window [999, 1002] which merges with [1000, 1003] to form [999, 1003]. This merged window also isn't late. When the watermark reaches 1004 and a new element arrives at T=1005 with timestamp 1002.5, it creates window [1002.5, 1005.5], but this doesn't overlap with [999, 1003] so no further merging occurs.\n\nActually, I need to reconsider the session window merging logic. Windows merge if their gap is ≤ 3, and since [999, 1003] and [1002.5, 1005.5] have a gap of -0.5 (they actually overlap), they should merge into [999, 1005.5]. The merged window passes the lateness check. At T=1006, the watermark advances and an element with timestamp 1000 arrives, creating [1000, 1003], which should merge with the existing [999, 1005.5] based on their gap.\n\nAt T=1007, the window [999, 1005.5] becomes late since 1005.5 + 0 ≤ 1007. When another element arrives at T=1008 with timestamp 1000, it would create [1000, 1003], but the question is whether the original merged window is still available for merging or has already been retired. If it's still in the merging set, they'd merge into [999, 1005.5], which would then fail the lateness check and be skipped.\n\nThe element itself would be marked as late (1000 + 0 ≤ 1008) and sent to the side output. This behavior appears correct based on the current implementation. However, there's a potential issue: the task description mentions the bug is checking lateness before merging, yet the code seems to check after. This suggests either the code has already been corrected, or the description is outlining what the fix should be rather than the current buggy state.\n\nLooking more carefully at the logic, I notice the comment on lines 435-439 describes the lateness condition differently than what `isElementLate` actually implements. The comment states the check should verify \"current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than element timestamp,\" but the actual method may be evaluating this condition incorrectly or at the wrong point in the processing pipeline.\n\nThe real issue appears to be with merging windows specifically. When a late element arrives and gets assigned to a window that then merges with an existing window, the merged window gets retired without the element being processed. This means the element never reaches the window operator, yet the code still needs to determine whether to emit it to the side output. The problem is that `isSkippedElement` becomes true and `isElementLate` is checked, but this happens after the window has already been merged and retired, potentially missing the correct lateness evaluation for the merged result.\n\nLooking back at the task description more carefully—it mentions the lateness check happens BEFORE merging, which would cause an element to be incorrectly classified as late even though it could extend a session window. But examining the actual code flow, the lateness check appears to occur after merging. sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(CountTrigger.of(4)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 6500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // add an element that merges the two \"key1\" sessions, they should now have count 6, and\n        // therefore fire\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 10), 4500));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-22\", 10L, 10000L), 9999L));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n elements to \"point windows\",\n     * windows that have the same timestamp for start and end.\n     *\n     * <p>In this test, elements that have 33 as the second tuple field will be put into a point\n     * window.\n     */\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testPointSessions() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot;\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n            testHarness.open();\n\n            // add elements out-of-order\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 1000));\n\n            // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n            snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        }\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n            testHarness.setup();\n            testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n            testHarness.open();\n\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 2500));\n\n testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 33), 2500));\n\n            testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n            expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-36\", 10L, 4000L), 3999));\n            expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-67\", 0L, 3000L), 2999));\n            expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n            TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                    \"Output was not correct.\",\n                    expectedOutput,\n                    testHarness.getOutput(),\n                    new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static <OUT>\n            OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> createTestHarness(\n                    OneInputStreamOperatorFactory<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> operator) {\n        try {\n            return new KeyedOneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<>(\n                    operator, new TupleKeySelector(), BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO);\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            throw new RuntimeException(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static <OUT>\n            OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> createAsyncTestHarness(\n                    OneInputStreamOperator<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> operator) {\n        try {\n            return AsyncKeyedOneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness.create(\n                    operator, new TupleKeySelector(), BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO);\n        } catch (Exception e) {\n            throw new RuntimeException(e);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testContinuousWatermarkTrigger(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        GlobalWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                GlobalWindows.create(),\n                                new GlobalWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, GlobalWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                ContinuousEventTimeTrigger.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, GlobalWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                                GlobalWindows.create(),\n                                null, /*Required async trigger*/\n                                new ExecutionConfig(),\n                                STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                TypeInformation.of(String.class))\n                        .asyncTrigger(\n                                AsyncContinuousEventTimeTrigger.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // The global window actually ignores these timestamps...\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 0));\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 20));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n 2000L;\n                                    }\n                                default:\n                                    return 0L;\n                            }\n                        });\n\n                   stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n Watermark(8999));\n\n        // test gap when it produces an end time before current timeout\n        // the furthest timeout is respected\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 9000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 10000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 13000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 10), 13500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(16999));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-21\", 13000L, 16000L), 15999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimeOverflow(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 1000;\n        final long lateness = 2000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        TumblingEventTimeWindows windowAssigner =\n                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofMillis(windowSize));\n\n        final WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                windowAssigner,\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        windowAssigner,\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        long timestamp = Long.MAX_VALUE - 1750;\n        Collection<TimeWindow> windows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1),\n                        timestamp,\n                        new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                            @Override\n                            public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                                return enableAsyncState\n                                        ? ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .getWindowAssignerContext()\n                                                .getCurrentProcessingTime()\n                                        : ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>)\n                                                        testHarness.getOperator())\n                                                .windowAssignerContext.getCurrentProcessingTime();\n                            }\n                        });\n        TimeWindow window = Iterables.getOnlyElement(windows);\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), timestamp));\n\n        // the garbage collection timer would wrap-around\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(window.maxTimestamp());\n\n        // and it would prematurely fire with watermark (Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500)\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp() + lateness).isLessThan(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500);\n\n        // if we don't correctly prevent wrap-around in the garbage collection\n        // timers this watermark will clean our window state for the just-added\n        // element/window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n\n        // this watermark is before the end timestamp of our only window\n        assertThat(window.maxTimestamp()).isStrictlyBetween(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500, Long.MAX_VALUE);\n\n        // push in a watermark that will trigger computation of our window\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(Long.MAX_VALUE - 1500));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), window.maxTimestamp()));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(window.maxTimestamp()));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> sideExpected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be dropped because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // this is sideoutput as late, reuse last timestamp\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n        sideExpected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 14500L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"SideOutput was not correct.\",\n                sideExpected,\n                (Iterable) sideActual,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testDropDueToLatenessSessionWithLatenessPurgingTrigger() throws Exception {\n\n        // this has the same     // same as testSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionWithLateness() but with an accumulating\n        // trigger, i.e.\n        // one that does not return FIRE_AND_PURGE when firing but just FIRE. The expected\n        // results are therefore slightly different.\n\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // because of the small allowed lateness and because the trigger is accumulating\n        // this will be merged into the session (11600-14600) and therefore will not\n        // be sideoutput as late\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n\n        // adding (\"key2\", 1) extended the session to (10000-146000) for which\n        // maxTimestamp <= currentWatermark. Therefore, we immediately get a firing\n        // with the current version of EventTimeTrigger/EventTimeTriggerAccum\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-2\", 10000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-3\", 10000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows2(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 100;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        String,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction2()),\n                                new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> testHarness =\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new PassThroughFunction2()))\n                        : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2100));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(\"GOT: (key2,1)\", 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2100)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    private static class PassThroughFunction2\n            implements WindowFunction<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<String> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            out.collect(\"GOT: \" + Joiner.on(\",\").join(input));\n        }\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void                      windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    //  UDFs\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    private static class PassThroughFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> in : input) {\n                out.collect(in);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class EmptyReturnFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {}\n    }\n\n    private static class SumReducer implements ReduceFunction<Tuple2<String, Integer>> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public Tuple2<String, Integer> reduce(\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> value1, Tuple2<String, Integer> value2) throws Exception {\n            return new Tuple2<>(value2.f0, value1.f1 + value2.f1);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class RichSumReducer<W extends Window>\n            extends RichWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, W> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private boolean openCalled = false;\n\n        @Override\n        public void open(OpenContext openContext) throws Exception {\n            super.open(openContext);\n            openCalled = true;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void close() throws Exception {\n            super.close();\n            closeCalled.incrementAndGet();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                W window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n\n            assertThat(openCalled).as(\"Open was not called\").isTrue();\n\n            int sum = 0;\n\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> t : input) {\n                sum += t.f1;\n            }\n            out.collect(new Tuple2<>(key, sum));\n        }\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private static class Tuple2ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    return sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private static class Tuple3ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    comparison = (int) (sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1);\n                    if (comparison != 0) {\n                        return comparison;\n                    }\n                    return (int) (sr0.getValue().f2 - sr1.getValue().f2);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class TupleKeySelector implements KeySelector<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> {\n }\n\n    private static class SessionWindowFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            int sum = 0;\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> i : values) {\n                sum += i.f1;\n            }\n            String resultString = key + \"-\" + sum;\n            out.collect(new Tuple3<>(resultString, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class ReducedSessionWindowFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, PointSessionWindows(long sessionTimeout) {\n            super(sessionTimeout);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        public Collection<TimeWindow> assignWindows(\n                Object element, long timestamp, WindowAssignerContext ctx) {\n            if (element instanceof Tuple2) {\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> t2 = (Tuple2<String, Integer>) element;\n                if (t2.f1 == 33) {\n                    return Collections.singletonList(new TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp));\n                }\n            }\n of the fired\n     * window. This is to test the state garbage collection mechanism.\n     */\n    private static class EventTimeTriggerAccumGC extends Trigger<Object, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private long cleanupTime;\n\n        public EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(long cleanupTime) {\n            this.cleanupTime = cleanupTime;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onElement(\n                Object element, long timestamp, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            if (window.maxTimestamp() <= ctx.getCurrentWatermark()) {\n                // if the watermark is already past the window fire immediately\n                return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n            } else {\n                ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n                return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) {\n            return time == window.maxTimestamp() || time == window.maxTimestamp() + cleanupTime\n                    ? TriggerResult.FIRE_AND_PURGE\n                    : TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void clear(TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) throws Exception {\n            ctx.deleteEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean canMerge() {\n            return true;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void onMerge(TimeWindow window, OnMergeContext ctx) {\n            ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"EventTimeTrigger()\";\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class FireEverytimeOnElementAndEventTimeTrigger\n            extends Trigger<Tuple2<String, Integer>, TimeWindow> {\n window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void clear(TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) throws Exception {}\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":3971,"oldLines":4,"newStart":3971,"newLines":74,"lines":["         @Override","         public void clear(TimeWindow window, Before we determine whether a window is late, we should first check if the element could be merged into an existing non-expired window. The requirement is clear: late elements in merging windows need to verify they can't be added to any active window before being marked as late.\n\nThe fix involves checking whether the merged window resulted from combining with an existing valid window. If so, we shouldn't discard it purely because the merged result is now late. A simpler approach might be to let the trigger mechanism handle cleanup for merging windows rather than skipping based on lateness alone.\n\nThe core issue is that after `addWindow()` completes, the code checks if the window is late and retires it without distinguishing between newly created windows and those formed through merging. We need to identify whether a window came from a merge operation and handle it differently—perhaps by checking if the window already existed in the merging set before the operation. existing (non-expired) window before marking as late.\n\nI need to check if the element could merge with an existing non-expired window prior to treating it as late. Before calling `mergingWindows.addWindow()`, I should verify whether any existing windows in the merging set could absorb this new window. The fix involves adjusting when we apply the late window check—we should only retire the window if it truly can't merge with anything still active. the window state is actually managed by the state backend rather than the WindowOperator itself—retiring just removes it from the in-flight set. The real problem seems to be that when skipping windows due to lateness, we're not immediately sending the element to the side output. Instead, we check `isSkippedElement` at the end of the method, which only triggers if that flag is true. So if all windows get skipped, the flag stays true and the element should be emitted to the side output at the end. One issue could be with how windows are retired from the merging set—specifically, if we call `retireWindow()` on a window that was just merged, it might not exist in the set anymore and could cause an exception.\n\nLooking at the `retireWindow()` method, it removes the window from the mapping and throws an `IllegalStateException` if the window isn't found. But if a merged window is properly added to the mapping, this shouldn't be the problem.\n\nThe real issue seems to be about the order of operations: before marking an element as late, I need to check whether it could merge with an existing non-expired window in the merging set. The current code probably checks if the window is late first, which causes valid mergeable elements to be skipped.\n\nThe fix requires modifying the lateness check to exclude windows that are actively merging with non-late windows. I could track whether a merge occurred by setting a flag during the merge callback, then checking that flag before applying the late element logic.\n\nUsing a wrapper array to capture the merge state during `addWindow()` would work—if `merged[0]` is true, the window resulted from merging and shouldn't be skipped even if it's late. However, this approach might miss cases where a late window merges with an existing non-late window, since the merge callback fires but the actual window reference might already be established.\n\nThe real issue is checking whether an element can merge with an existing non-expired window *before* deciding it's too late. For session windows specifically, a late element that extends an active session should still be processed. So I need to check if any existing windows could accept this element through merging, and only mark it late if no such non-expired windows exist. This means examining the merge candidates before calling `addWindow()`.\n\nThe challenge is that checking merge compatibility is expensive. A simpler approach: track whether the window returned from `addWindow()` represents a merge of existing windows versus a brand new window. Only skip late elements that create entirely new windows, not those that extend existing ones. I could return merge information from the callback itself to determine this.\n\nLooking at the existing code, there's already a late window check in the merge callback that throws an exception if merging would produce a late window. This happens during `addWindow()`, so the returned window shouldn't be late if the merge succeeded. Yet there's still a redundant `isWindowLate()` check afterward that seems unnecessary given this earlier validation.\n\nTracing through the flow: when an element arrives and gets assigned to windows, `addWindow()` is called for each one. If merging occurs, the callback validates the result isn't late and throws if it is. The only way `actualWindow` could be late after this is if it's a brand new window with no merge involved. The redundant check at line 369 catches these new late windows, but the merge path should already be protected by the earlier exception.\n\nHowever, re-reading the task more carefully—it mentions the bug involves checking lateness BEFORE merging, which would cause elements that could extend a session window to be incorrectly marked as late. The current code structure checks after merging, suggesting either the issue has been partially addressed or there's a different problem at play with how the side output emission interacts with the merging logic. a fix based on my understanding of what the task is asking for: \"Ensure late elements for merging windows check whether the element could be merged into an existing (non-expired) window BEFORE marking as late\"\n\nFor merging windows, I should only skip processing if the element is late AND it wouldn't merge with any existing non-expired windows. Instead of immediately checking `isWindowLate(actualWindow)`, I need to check if the merge callback was actually invoked. If it was, the element successfully merged with an existing window and should be processed regardless of lateness. I'll skip it only when no merge occurred and the window is late.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":7,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2468,"cache_read_input_tokens":68472,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2468},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f3eab87a-9d2f-419a-a643-4f9ae6cfb7cd","uuid":"03ccc43b-6a0d-40ec-a94c-4fc309d8e048"}

logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n * Trigger}.\n *\n * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n * WindowAssigner}.\n *\n * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n        implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Configuration values and user functions\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n\n    private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n\n    private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n\n    /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n\n    /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n\n    /**\n     * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n     *\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n     *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n     *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n     * </ul>\n     */\n    protected final long allowedLateness;\n\n    /**\n     * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n     * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n     * to this.\n     */\n    protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n\n    private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n\n    protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that is not checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n    private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n\n    /**\n     * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n     * state is not mergeable.\n     */\n    private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n\n    /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n    private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n\n    /**\n     * This is given to the {@code InternalWindowFunction} for emitting elements with a given\n     * timestamp.\n     */\n    protected transient TimestampedCollector<OUT> timestampedCollector;\n\n    protected transient Context triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n\n    protected transient WindowContext processContext;\n\n    protected transient WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext windowAssignerContext;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that needs to be checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n\n    /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n    public WindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n        super(windowFunction);\n\n        checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n\n        checkArgument(\n                windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n                \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n\n        this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n        this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n        this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\n        this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\n        this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\n        this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\n        this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\n        this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setup(\n            StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\n            StreamConfig config,\n            Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\n        super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\n        super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n        timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n\n        internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\"window-timers\", windowSerializer, this);\n\n        triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n        processContext = new WindowContext(null);\n\n        windowAssignerContext =\n                new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                    @Override\n                    public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                        return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                    }\n                };\n\n        // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\n        // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\n        if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\n            windowState =\n                    (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\n        }\n\n        // create the typed and helper states But wait, there's still an issue with the logic flow. Let me re-think this...\n\nActually, I think the right fix is different. Looking at the requirement again: \"Ensure late elements for merging windows check whether the element could be merged into an existing (non-expired) window BEFORE marking as late\"\n\nThis means: if the element merges with an existing non-late window, we should process the element even if the merged window is late.\n\nBut how do we know if the merge is with a non-late window? In the merge callback, we don't have information about whether the existing windows were late.\n\nActually, I think my current fix is correct as a first step. If merging occurred, the element was trying to extend an existing window, so it should be emitted to side output rather than silently dropped.\n\nBut wait, looking at the logic more carefully, there's still the issue of the final check at line 440. If we already emit to side output here, we shouldn't also emit it at the final check. Let me adjust the logic.\n\nActually, let me reconsider the whole approach. The issue might be that we're handling this in the wrong place. Let me look at the final check again:\n\n```java\nif (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n    if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n        sideOutput(element);    } else {\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThis only emits to side output if `isSkippedElement` is true. But if we're emitting in the merging window case, we should mark that the element was handled so that we don't emit it twice.\n\nActually, looking at my code again, I'm already setting the continue statement after emitting to side output, which should prevent it from being handled elsewhere.\n\n\nLet me trace through the three scenarios: when a merge happens on a late window, we emit to side output and skip further processing; when a new window is late, we skip and let the final check handle the side output; when the window isn't late, we proceed normally. The key is ensuring we don't double-emit by using continue statements strategically. the window, I should still process it normally and let the element be added to state. Then, after all windows have been processed, if the element wasn't handled by any window, emit it to side output.\n\nThis approach ensures elements are added to their windows even when those windows are late, while still capturing truly unhandled elements in the side output.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":6,"cache_creation_input_tokens":6479,"cache_read_input_tokens":70940,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":6479},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f3eab87a-9d2f-419a-a643-4f9ae6cfb7cd","uuid":"d6a178b4-e785-4330-b6ff-1afa58dcc59a"}

java.util.Collection;\n\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n\n/**\n * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n * Trigger}.\n *\n * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n * WindowAssigner}.\n *\n * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n        implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Configuration values and user functions\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n\n    private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n\n    private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n\n    /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n\n    /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n\n    /**\n     * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n     *\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n     *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n     *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n     * </ul>\n     */\n    protected final long allowedLateness;\n\n    /**\n     * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n     * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n     * to this.\n     */\n    protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n\n    private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n\n    protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that is not checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n    private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n\n    /**\n     * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n     * state is not mergeable.\n     */\n    private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n\n    /** The state that holds the merging window on the given policies and user functions. */\n    public WindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n
